document: REVISED REGULATORY FRAMEWORK FOR BANK VERIFICATION NUMBER (BVN) OPERATIONS AND WATCH-LIST FOR THE NIGERIAN BANKING INDUSTRY 121021 FINAL (Nigeria - Row 11)

sections.title: BVN system participants and their roles in banking operations
sections.summary: This section defines the regulatory framework for Bank Verification Number (BVN) operations and Watch-list in Nigeria. It specifies the roles and responsibilities of Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN), Nigeria Inter-Bank Settlement System Plc (NIBSS), banks, other financial institutions, and customers. The framework mandates BVN enrollment, identification, verification, issuance, linking to accounts, and delinking processes. It also outlines procedures for watch-listing breaches, fraud management, and customer information updates. Participants must ensure BVN data is stored within Nigeria, not routed across borders without CBN consent, and comply with data protection regulations.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes Nigeria's BVN system with strict data residency requirements (BVN data must be stored within Nigeria) and cross-border restrictions (foreign entities cannot access BVN database). It mandates specific procedures for watch-listing breaches and delisting, which are critical for assessing data transfer risks when handling BVN information across borders.

requirements.text: Central Bank of Nigeria shall review and approve the regulatory Framework and Standard Operating Guidelines
Central Bank of Nigeria shall approve eligible users for access to BVN information
Central Bank of Nigeria shall approve access to BVN information
Central Bank of Nigeria shall ensure objectives of BVN initiatives are fully achieved
Central Bank of Nigeria shall monitor stakeholders to ensure compliance
Central Bank of Nigeria shall apply sanctions for non-compliance
Central Bank of Nigeria shall conduct oversight and operate the BVN system
Central Bank of Nigeria shall approve requests for delisting from Watch-list
Central Bank of Nigeria shall mediate issues arising from Watch-list between participants
Central Bank of Nigeria shall handle consumer complaints on BVN
NIBSS shall collaborate with stakeholders to develop and review Standard Operating Guidelines
NIBSS shall ensure seamless operations of BVN system
NIBSS shall maintain BVN database
NIBSS shall manage access to BVN information by approved users
NIBSS shall ensure recourse to CBN on any request for BVN information
NIBSS shall ensure adequate security of BVN information
NIBSS shall update Watch-list with BVNs of enlisted individuals
NIBSS shall use Watch-list report submitted by participants to delist BVN
NIBSS shall provide participants with portal for verification of watch-listed BVN
NIBSS shall provide Application Programming Interface for eligible institutions
NIBSS shall keep audit trail of activities on Watch-list
NIBSS shall put in place Service Level Agreement with stakeholders
NIBSS shall provide CBN access to Watch-list
Banks and OFIs shall ensure proper capturing of BVN data and validate before linkage
Banks and OFIs shall link all operated accounts/wallets (except Tier 1) with BVNs within 24 hours
Banks and OFIs shall enroll mobile money wallets (except Tier 1) on BVN within 180 days
Banks and OFIs shall link BVN details of signatories, directors, and beneficial owners
Banks and OFIs shall ensure customer's name on BVN matches across accounts
Banks and OFIs shall report BVNs of individuals in breach to NIBSS within 1 business day
Banks and OFIs shall report BVNs of deceased customers to NIBSS within 24 hours
Banks and OFIs shall use Watch-list report to delist BVN
Customers shall provide accurate biometrics and biodata
Customers shall report suspicious or unauthorized activities on accounts

sections.source_quote: The Central Bank of Nigeria, in collaboration with the Bankers' Committee, deployed a centralised Bank Verification Number (BVN) System in February 2014.

sections.heading: (continued)
sections.title: Penalties for BVN watch-list breaches
sections.summary: Participants failing to meet BVN framework obligations face penalties from the Central Bank of Nigeria. Customers with watch-listed BVNs cannot open new accounts, may lose electronic banking access, and are restricted from credit facilities. Penalties apply to all accounts linked to watch-listed BVNs except Tier 1 accounts. Recurring breaches extend penalty periods consecutively.
sections.dtia_summary: Penalties for watch-listed BVNs restrict cross-border data transfers by prohibiting electronic channel access and credit facilities for affected customers, requiring DTIA to assess impact on financial transactions.

requirements.text: Customers with watch-listed BVNs cannot open new relationships with any participant
Participants may choose to terminate business relationships with watch-listed account holders (except Tier 1)
Watch-listed account holders (except Tier 1) cannot use electronic channels including ATM, POS, internet banking, mobile banking, USSD, or issue third-party cheques
Watch-listed account holders (except Tier 1) cannot reference accounts, access or guarantee credit facilities
Watch-listed customers remain on the watch-list for periods specified in Table 2
Recurrence of breaches extends penalty periods consecutively
Penalties apply to all accounts/wallets (except Tier 1) linked to watch-listed BVNs

sections.source_quote: Any participant who fails to perform its stipulated responsibilities shall be penalised by the Central Bank of Nigeria.
